  ACL, supersession links. Chunks become derived, regenerable artifacts.
  This concept locks that framing for JOS: every knowledge document is
  a tracked, governed, durable operating-surface artifact. Foundry CIP
  (the canonical multi-tenant KB host per JOSW2 Group F) ingests these
  governed docs; chunks are downstream plumbing.

# Knowledge as First-Class Artifact

## The unit of knowledge is the document, not the chunk

When a knowledge base is ingested into a retrieval system (RAG /
GraphRAG), the source documents get fragmented into chunks for
embedding. The naive failure mode treats those chunks as the unit —
they're stored, indexed, retrieved, and the document they came from
becomes incidental. This breaks three things:

1. **Freshness** — a chunk in a vector store has no freshness signal.
   The document it came from might be 18 months stale; the chunk
   still ranks first in retrieval.
2. **Provenance** — when an agent surfaces a fact, "where did this
   come from?" routes back to a chunk_id, not a document with an
   owner, source URL, last-verified date. Auditing breaks.
3. **Supersession** — knowledge changes over time. A document may
   be superseded by a newer version. Without supersession links at
   the document level, the old chunks live forever in the index.

The fix is structural: **make the document the first-class artifact,
not the chunk**.

## What "first-class document" means

A knowledge document in JOS is a **governed operating-surface
artifact** with:

- **Manifest / frontmatter** — declares the document (per [JOS-S34]
  knowledge-document-schema): `asset_id`, `domain`, `asset_type`,
  `version`, `status`, `owner`, etc.
- **Version** — semantic `MAJOR.MINOR`. Bumps on substantive content
  change (not formatting).
- **Freshness signal** — `last_verified` + `last_verified_by` +
  `freshness_sla_days`. Documents past SLA emit drift warnings.
- **Provenance** — `source_urls`, `created_by`, `verified_by`,
  `produced_from` (if the doc was promoted from a WORKBENCH file).
- **ACL / visibility** — declared scope: `tenant_id`, optional
  `audience` (e.g., agent-only, human-only, mixed).
- **Supersession links** — `supersedes:` and `superseded_by:` fields
  for explicit retire / replace chains.

Chunks become a **derived, regenerable artifact** of the document:
- Chunks belong to a `source_id` (CIP `knowledge_sources` row)
- Chunks inherit the document's metadata via tagging
- When the document changes, chunks are re-ingested (Foundry CIP's
  re-ingest hook handles this)
- When the document is superseded, old chunks are flagged
  `validation_status: superseded` or removed

The chunk is not the unit of truth. The document is.

## Why this works at the JOS scale

Project Silk's `asset-metadata-schema v1.2` (declared venture-agnostic
in its `applies_to:` field — Silk, Ecomlever, Rocky Ridge, Shatcher
ventures) is the reference implementation of this concept. Its
schema carries:

- Universal fields (asset_type, domain, asset_id, version, status,
  created, updated, owner)
- Coverage (`covers_slots` — which standard slots this doc covers)
- Tier-1 Discoverability (`last_verified, source_urls, freshness_sla_days`)
- Tier-2 Structural (`disposition`, `dependencies`, `exceptions`,
  `vendor`)
- KB-specific (`category, kb_tier, china_panel_status, volatility`)

When this concept ratifies, [JOS-S34] codifies the schema as JOS
canonical (bubbling Silk's v1.2 UP per the no-information-loss
canonicalization rule from JOSW2 Group B Item 13).

## Authoring + ratification flow

Knowledge documents are **authored in venture (build-time activity)**
and **promoted to operating-surface via ratification**:

1. Researcher / agent drafts knowledge content in `WORKBENCH/<person>/
   proj-<X>/` with `doc_kind: research` or `discovery`.
2. When content matures, promote via [JOS-METHOD-AUTHOR-A-KNOWLEDGE-
   DOCUMENT]: apply [JOS-S34] frontmatter, pick domain + asset_type,
   place in venture's `.jos/knowledge/<domain>/<asset_type>/`.
3. CIP ingestion hook (per JOSW2 Group F Item 47) re-indexes the
   document into Foundry CIP.
4. Document is now retrievable via `foundry_mcp_knowledge_search`
   with tenant_id + domain filters.

**The document lives in the venture repo** (canonical MD with
frontmatter). **CIP holds the indexed/retrievable form** (chunks +
embeddings + BM25 index). On change, re-ingest. The venture repo is
the source of truth; CIP is a projection.

## What changes about retrieval governance

Because the document is first-class, retrieval can now answer:

- "What's the freshness of the source this fact came from?" (check
  `last_verified` + `freshness_sla_days`)
- "Who owns this fact?" (`owner` field)
- "What other documents does this depend on?" (`dependencies:` field
  per Silk Tier-2)
- "Has this been superseded?" (`superseded_by:` field)
- "What tenant / domain does this belong to?" (already in CIP schema
  via `tenant_id` + `domain_tags`)
- "Where is the source URL?" (`source_urls:` field)

Naive chunk-RAG can't answer any of these. Document-first-class RAG
can.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-S34]** — Knowledge-Document Schema (the manifest fields this
  concept declares as required)
- **[JOS-S35]** — Knowledge-Organization Standard (5-level maturity
  + domain pattern for organizing knowledge docs)
- **[JOS-METHOD-AUTHOR-A-KNOWLEDGE-DOCUMENT]** — Procedure for
  creating a properly-shaped knowledge doc
- **[JOS-METHOD-INGEST-VENTURE-KB-INTO-CIP]** — Procedure for
  ingestion into Foundry CIP
- **[JOS-C47]** — Build-Surface vs Operating-Surface (knowledge docs
  live on operating-surface; authoring happens in WORKBENCH)
- **[JOS-D0066]** — Doc-Type × Thing-Type Matrix (knowledge docs are
  operating-surface artifacts)
- **Foundry CIP `knowledge_sources` + `knowledge_chunks` tables** —
  the indexed form

## Drift-prevention

- **Don't treat chunks as the unit of knowledge.** They're derived
  from documents. Documents are tracked; chunks are regenerated.
- **Don't ingest documents without frontmatter.** Without
  manifest, the document can't be governed (per [JOS-C45]).
- **Don't author venture-side knowledge docs that diverge from the
  schema.** Use [JOS-S34] (which generalizes Silk v1.2). Schema
  evolution goes through outbox.
- **Don't skip freshness signals.** `last_verified` + `freshness_sla_
  days` are required; without them, documents rot silently in the
  index.
- **Don't keep the source-of-truth in CIP.** CIP is a projection.
  The venture's repo MD with frontmatter is canonical.
- **Don't ingest WORKBENCH content directly.** WORKBENCH lives on
  build-surface; only promoted operating-surface knowledge docs are
  ingestion targets.

## Academic / Industry Backing

The shift from chunk-first-class to document-first-class RAG is the
explicit direction of 2026 enterprise knowledge architecture:

- **Atlan** ("Enterprise RAG Platforms Comparison 2026") describes
  the shift from "vector store with chunks" to "governed knowledge
  fabric with document manifests + provenance."
- **NStarX** ("The Next Frontier of RAG 2026-2030") names "knowledge
  document as first-class governed artifact" as the convergent
  direction.
- **arXiv 2512.05411** ("Systematic Framework for Enterprise
  Knowledge Retrieval") argues for LLM-generated metadata enrichment
  at ingest as standard, presupposing the document carries metadata
  to enrich.
- **LangChain `Document` / LlamaIndex `Node` schemas** — both have
  `metadata: dict` as a first-class field on the document object;
  chunks inherit metadata from the parent document.
- **Pinecone official multitenancy guidance (2026)** — namespace per
  tenant, metadata-rich documents, hybrid retrieval — assumes
  per-document metadata is rich enough to filter on.

The recurring observation: **systems that treat the chunk as the
unit lose the ability to govern knowledge at meaningful timescales.**
Document-as-unit recovers it.
